name: Lung Cancer
summary: Lung cancer risk is strongly influenced by inhaled exposures (especially smoking) and has gene–environment components in population studies. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Rare in adolescence; included to illustrate exposure-driven carcinogenesis and GxE reasoning at a population level.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Smoking introduces carcinogens and promotes chronic inflammation and DNA damage context.
Ambient particulates associated with lung cancer outcomes in some studies.
Occupational particulates are context-dependent inhaled exposures.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many lung cancer GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS and locus mapping can vary with ancestry and exposure distribution.
population_equity.data_gaps: Separating genetic effects from exposure mediation is challenging.

references.title: Genome-wide association meta-analysis identifies lung cancer susceptibility loci
The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

references.title: Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association

references.title: Occupational exposures and chronic lung disease risk: overview (review)
